block: X-C.1 footprint-wide nonprofit health-system 990 contractor sweep
transport_keyword_regex: ambulanc|medical transport|patient transport|transport(?:ation)? of patients|\bems\b|emergency medical|air medical|life ?flight|med ?flight|medevac|medivac|paramedic|ambulette|american medical response|\bamr\b|ambulnz|\bnemt\b|critical care transport|mobile intensive care|superior air|midwest medical transport|transport
method_notes: Seed universe: 40+ large nonprofit health systems operating hospitals in the footprint states (NE IA KS MO OH WI VA MN IN KY), enumerated from the HCRIS hospital roster (recognizable multi-hospital system families) BEYOND the 9 cohort systems already swept in cohort_990.json (Cleveland Clinic, Inova, CommonSpirit/CHI Health NE, Nebraska Methodist, Premier, Saint Luke's KC, Froedtert, Baptist Health KY, Ascension). Public/state-authority systems and for-profit chains excluded. Main filer EIN resolved via ProPublica Nonprofit Explorer API v2 search (filtered by state and name, foundation/health-plan/auxiliary filers excluded, highest-revenue matching filer chosen; one main filer per system, runner-up candidates recorded). Latest two XML-available Form 990s per filer; object_ids scraped from the public ProPublica organization page; XML fetched from the GivingTuesday 990 data-lake public S3 mirror (gt990datalake-rawdata.s3.amazonaws.com), ProPublica download-xml as fallback. Part VII Section B parsed from IndependentContractorCompensationGrp / ContractorCompensationGrp (ContractorName, ServicesDesc, CompensationAmt) and CntrctRcvdGreaterThan100KCnt. INFORMATION FLOOR: Form 990 Part VII Section B discloses only the five highest-compensated independent contractors above $100,000, so a transport vendor billing below a filer's fifth-highest contractor never appears; contractors_total per filing is the count of contractors over $100,000. Transport screen: a contractor is kept when its disclosed name OR services string matches the ambulance/EMS/medical-transport regex; the verbatim service description is preserved so genuine transport operators can be distinguished from emergency-department physician-coverage groups the broad 'emergency medical' term also catches. Public filings; entries recorded verbatim; no share-of-wallet or relationship inference beyond the filing text.
